service: Maintain the City's water delivery and sewer collection systems.
oneLiner: This work matters because NYC's water travels through aqueducts and tunnels up to 125 miles long, some over a century old, and keeping that vast hidden network of pipes intact is what stands between New Yorkers and a water supply failure.
summary: New York City's water arrives through the Croton, Catskill and Delaware systems, engineering projects built between the 1840s and 1940s that remain in continuous use today, alongside a sewer collection network that covers most of the five boroughs. Because so much of this infrastructure predates modern construction standards, ongoing maintenance and repair, including major projects like the decades-long repair of the Delaware Aqueduct, is essential to preventing leaks, contamination and service disruptions for the roughly half of New York State's population that depends on this system.

sources.label: New York City water supply system - Wikipedia
